population_applicability: Partial: the baseline workflow starts at 3 months and spans adults and children; the two exact sources cover common cold broadly and fever specifically in children.
setting_applicability: Partial: both exact sources are limited to DHA-licensed telehealth services.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai and DHA-licensed telehealth only; broader UAE applicability is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official Version/Issue 2 copies were opened on 2026-07-11; each states a revision date of 2029-02-21.
superseded_check: DHA official portal checked on 2026-07-11; no newer official issue was identified there.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The reviewed 1. Background section is directly relevant to documenting Fever, subject to the recorded population and setting limitations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies severity-risk contexts and distinguishes uncomplicated common cold from other respiratory presentations.

evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth scope and emergency/controlled-medication exclusions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The reviewed 6. Red Flags section is directly relevant to documenting Fever, subject to the recorded population and setting limitations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists respiratory, swallowing, neurologic, rash, and pediatric breathing concerns that require assessment or escalation.

evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

unresolved_source_gaps: No single exact source covers the mixed-age, combined fever/URTI workflow across telehealth and in-person settings.
